summary: Update Convenient Transactions API to use Async Closures
description:
Rust v1.85 announced the support for [async closures|https://devblogs.microsoft.com/cosmosdb/announcing-the-public-preview-of-the-azure-cosmos-db-sdk-for-rust/?_bhlid=65f8d90acc68258258e8aa42f1dfc4b3b8528e7e] which will return futures when called. This can help us simplify our current iteration of the [Convenient Transactions API|https://docs.rs/mongodb/latest/mongodb/action/struct.StartTransaction.html#method.and_run] by making is simpler and more readable. This includes but is not limited to changes like removal of `my_data` field and the use of `.boxed()`  

*A/C*
 * Existing API can be marked deprecated until next major version
 * Allow users to use this newer version of the API 

[*Reference*|https://rust-trends.com/posts/rust-s-async-closures/]

summary: Remove human_readable option from [De]SerializerOptions
description: The utility type {{HumanReadable}} provides the same control with more granularity

summary: Drop uuid 0.8 support
description: Now that the {{uuid}} crate has reached 1.0, we should drop support for the 0.8 version and {{uuid-0_8}} feature flag we have.

summary: Support optionally using bson 3.0 in the driver
OIDC tests are broken
description: Looks like we were relying on the implicit credentials that were removed for DRIVERS-3188.

summary: Future-proof bson crate features
description: Similar to RUST-1208, we should clean up the set of crate features exposed and apply the {{compat}} future-proofing technique to the {{bson}} crate.

summary: Merge append and append_ref
description: RawDocumentBuf has append and append_ref for adding owned and borrowed values respectively; we might be able to merge these via use of conversion traits.

summary: Do not include NaN doubles in fuzzer tests
description: The BSON fuzzer tests failed [here|https://spruce.mongodb.com/task/rust_bson_fuzz_run_fuzzer_patch_fdbf863ab5531ade49c09a27019b32e3573ad437_681ce03fb4f3b80007b1b161_25_05_08_16_48_00/logs?execution=0] due to a document containing f64::NAN. Equality comparisons for f64::NAN will always fail because f64::NAN does not equal itself. We should update the fuzzer not to include NaN double values.

summary: bson: make time large-dates feature optional
description:
(From [this|https://github.com/mongodb/bson-rust/issues/475] github issue)

The bson crate needs the {{large-dates}} feature of the {{time}} crate to pass the Y10K corpus test; however, that feature causes a performance loss and introduces parsing ambiguities.  We should make it optional for users that know they won't be handling dates with years outside the ±9999 range.

summary: Remove lossy utf8 support in Serde deserialization
description:
Specifically, remove it in favor of making the internal {{value_utf8_lossy}} into a public API, which by itself will cover the need for working with a document with possibly-corrupted string values.

When we do this, we will need to revisit whether {{value_utf8_lossy}} should return:
* An enum with a restricted set of values as it currently does, or a full {{Bson}} enum
* Always-owned values as it currently does, or some flavor of {{CoW}} to avoid copying when no invalid utf8 is found.

summary: Implement From<std::net::SocketAddr> for ServerAddress
description:
See this issue for a related request: https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-rust-driver/issues/1203

The SocketAddr type maps cleanly to our ServerAddress::Tcp variant. We should implement the From trait so that users can work with SocketAddr more easily.

summary: Update RawElement::len name
description: The RawElement type has a len method that returns the number of bytes in the element. We should update the name to size, since the value it returns is the total size of one object and rather than a count of discrete items.

summary: Potential bug with underscore in cluster name in srv string
description:
A customer is reporting the following error using schema manager, which uses the rust driver:---Seedlist error:
Error: Driver Error Kind: An error occurred during DNS resolution: proto error: Label contains invalid characters: Err(Errors), labels: {}Mongos error:
Error: Kind: Server selection timeout: No available servers. Topology: \{ Type: Unknown, Servers: [ { Address: cud1c-004832.csdev.corp:30255, Type: Unknown, Error: Kind: I/O error: unexpected end of file, labels: {} } ] }, labels: {}They are using an srv style connection string, but their cluster name has an underscore somehow.


Created with Jira Integration+ for nathan.leniz (nathan.leniz@mongodb.com)

summary: BSON fuzzer failure
description:
Recent example: https://parsley.mongodb.com/evergreen/rust_bson_fuzz_run_fuzzer_patch_431d4483856b18d1b8885d0b46a60be7f2eb2dee_6862b2bf41b36c00070bedce_25_06_30_15_52_34/1/task?bookmarks=0,2761

It's the {{raw_deserialize_utf8_lossy}} target, and it always fails with an error of the form {{range start index 16 out of range for slice of length 15}} (although the indices vary).

The first commit that this failed on evergreen for is https://github.com/mongodb/bson-rust/commit/be2a10772e798a53b16938fb801d135c06c175bb, although it's a flaky failure so that's not 100% a smoking gun.

summary: URI options spec tests specify empty options when the input results in a non-empty option set
description: This ticket was split from {{DRIVERS-1368}}, please see that ticket for a detailed description.

summary: Drivers should unpin connections when ending a session
description: This ticket was split from {{DRIVERS-2647}}, please see that ticket for a detailed description.

summary: SDAM should prioritize electionId over setVersion only on >=6.0 servers
description: This ticket was split from {{DRIVERS-2412}}, please see that ticket for a detailed description.

summary: Bundle arguments to authenticate_stream
description:
Right now, any authentication mechanism that needs values from the client options needs a lot of plumbing; the data path goes {{ClientOptions}} -> {{HandshakerOptions}} -> {{Handshaker}} -> {{Credential::authenticate_stream}} -> {{AuthMechanism::authenticate_stream}} -> actual leaf function that uses the data.

Rather than having to update every layer each time a new value is introduced, we should introduce an {{AuthOptions}} struct that bundles them together; that will get constructed from {{ClientOptions}} and passed down into the leaf functions, and then anything new needed for auth mechanisms will only need to update the {{AuthOptions}} constructor and the point of use.

summary: Support auto encryption in unified tests
description: This ticket was split from {{DRIVERS-3106}}, please see that ticket for a detailed description.

summary: Use doc_auto_cfg for bson documentation
description:
We should make the change described in this driver PR comment in the bson library as well: https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-rust-driver/pull/823#issuecomment-1422975994

This should make documenting API behind feature flags more simple.

summary: Revisit serde representations of bson types
description:
(For backstory, see https://github.com/mongodb/bson-rust/pull/479)

Right now, the types provided by the {{bson}} crate will serialize to human-readable destinations in extjson format.  This is convenient because it gave us a ready-made list of representations and because if a bson type or user type embedding bson is serialized to json, it Just Works.

However, this has turned out to have some major issues:
* It's inefficient; for values backed by byte buffers we jump through a *lot* of hoops to make this work.
* It's lossy; extjson does not provide precise representations for various values, so serializing a document *into* a document can lose precision.
* It's hard to maintain; using extjson as internal representation means the number of places that need to parse or emit that internally has proliferated and are hard to adjust or fix.

As an alternative, we could explicitly document that the Serde representation of bson types is not guaranteed to be anything in particular, just that future releases will parse values emitted by older versions.  This would allow substantial code simplification, efficiency, and precision, and users that actually need extjson could use the already-provided explicit conversion functions (as Serde annotations or otherwise).

summary: Add top-level documentation for serde_helpers module
description: We should write top-level documentation for the serde_helpers module that explains how to use the new serde_with utilities.

summary: Fix handling of tlsInsecure in the URI
Client handshake metadata should be truncated when > 512 bytes long
description:
According to the spec (https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/mongodb-handshake/handshake.rst#limitations):

bq. The entire metadata BSON document MUST NOT exceed 512 bytes. This includes all BSON overhead. The client.application.name cannot exceed 128 bytes. MongoDB will return an error if these limits are not adhered to, which will result in handshake failure. Drivers MUST validate these values and truncate driver provided values if necessary. Implementors are encouraged to prioritize truncating the platform field before all others. Additionally, implementors are encouraged to place high priority information about the platform earlier in the string, in order to avoid possible truncating of those details.

We don't perform any of this truncation/validation in the driver currently, but we should as it will be a blocker for anyone who encounters it and there are no great workarounds. Note that fixing RUST-1268 is going to make it more likely users hit this issue.

summary: CreateCollectionOptions cannot deserialize double size field
description: See this issue for the bug report and investigation: https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-rust-driver/issues/1340#event-16927130308

summary: SCRAM-SHA-256 FIPS Compliance
description: This ticket was split from {{DRIVERS-2931}}, please see that ticket for a detailed description.

summary: Add has_next to cursor API
description:
User request: https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-rust-driver/issues/1258

We already have this method internally, so this would entail adding docs and tests.

summary: Lint for clippy::cast_sign_loss
description:
Right now we lint for {{clippy::cast_possible_truncation}} and {{clippy::cast_possible_wrap}} to catch errors that are likely to cause over/underflow or other severe bugs when handling data buffers; it turns out we should also be linting for {{clippy::cast_sign_loss}} in those cases where we're getting (for example) a buffer size as an {{i32}} and casting it to a {{usize}}.

It looks like we have a bunch of places that fail this lint, so this might not be a quick cleanup.

summary: "implementation of 'std::marker::Send' is not general enough" error when using and_run2
description: https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-rust-driver/issues/1490

summary: Implement Display and Serialize for ConnectionString
description: It would be helpful for users to be able to un-parse a {{ConnectionString}} back into a URL; the natural way to do that would be a {{Display}} impl, with {{Serialize}} as an added convenience.

summary: bson repo: fold serde-tests into normal tests
description: {{serde-tests}} is currently a distinct cargo project with local path dependency on bson; the library itself is blank and exists only to define tests.  AFAICT there's no particular reason for this to be in its own library, and it makes it easy to forget to update those tests when working on the {{bson}} crate proper.

summary: Add mongo.com to allowed hosts for OIDC
description: This ticket was split from {{DRIVERS-3192}}, please see that ticket for a detailed description.

summary: Derive Clone for ConnectionString
description: PR: [https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-rust-driver/pull/1546]

summary: Cache binaries for test suite runs on evergreen
description:
Currently, every evergreen task rebuilds test binaries fresh, even when a set of tasks are all running on the same architecture with the same flags.  We should investigate building once per arch*flags combo and fetching those artifacts in each task rather than rebuilding.

This has two benefits:
* it'll speed up task execution (compilation is not fast)
* it'll reduce load on cargo, which is increasingly becoming a [topic of concern|https://rustfoundation.org/media/rust-foundation-signs-joint-statement-on-open-source-infrastructure-stewardship/].

[AWS CodeArtifact|https://wiki.corp.mongodb.com/spaces/DBDEVPROD/pages/384992759/How+to+configure+your+project+to+use+AWS+CodeArtifact] might be an appropriate system to use for caching.

summary: Implement Default trait for CStr
description: user PR: https://github.com/mongodb/bson-rust/pull/625

summary: Error::custom should forward the error message to the enveloping type
description:
Right now if i create an error for example {{let err = mongotune_core::Error::MongoDBCommand(mongodb::error::Error::custom("Lorem ipsum".to_string()))}} then the "Lorem ipsum" does not show up on {{err.to_string()}}, just the "custom error" as message.
The {{mongodb::error::Error::custom}} should forward it's message when enveloped into an Error type.

summary: bson: expand integer conversions
description: See https://github.com/mongodb/bson-rust/issues/627.

summary: Search index management spec test incompatible with Local Atlas Docker image
description: This ticket was split from {{DRIVERS-3357}}, please see that ticket for a detailed description.

summary: Expose atClusterTime parameter in snapshot sessions
description: This ticket was split from {{DRIVERS-2782}}, please see that ticket for a detailed description.

summary: Implement a Bevy AssetReader
description: [Bevy|https://bevy.org/] is a game engine that has a lot of momentum in the Rust community.  Part of its API surface deals with loading assets (art, levels, etc.) from external sources; the core interface for this is [AssetReader|https://docs.rs/bevy/latest/bevy/asset/io/trait.AssetReader.html].  A wide variety of third-party implementations of this exist for things like loading over HTTP, reading from various archive formats, etc.  We could create a crate that does similar, with the backing store for assets being a MongoDB server.

summary: Append metadata tests fail with compression enabled
description: Example run: https://spruce.mongodb.com/task/mongo_rust_driver_compression_test_zlib_compression_11e4013d328e33c6e57429766540787118683fff_26_01_07_16_58_13/tests?execution=0&sorts=STATUS%3AASC

summary: Windows tests for mongod latest failing
description:
{{Command '['C:/data/mci/91e5/<REDACTED:DRIVERS_ATLAS_USER>-tools/mongodb/bin/mongod', '--version']' returned non-zero exit status 3221225785.}}

Once the server bug is fixed, we should re-enable these tests.

summary: Deprecate socketTimeoutMS from public and internal APIs
description: The driver does not support the socketTimeoutMS option; however, it is included as a public field on the ConnectionString type. It is also a pub(crate) field on ClientOptions. We should deprecate the public field and remove it in the next major version. We should also remove the internal ClientOptions field to avoid confusion when writing tests, as this option appears to be usable when setting client options from within the crate.

summary: retry_read_different_mongos test is flaky
description: Example failure: https://parsley.corp.mongodb.com/test/mongo_rust_driver_macos_14.00_test_8.0_sharded_patch_35370d3f93cc6ff9ebbd83487160342e7c149e7e_6960e464cd46fd0007eb35da_26_01_09_11_20_05/0/b8d3114321beb86f74ffc1f9d02a8d1e?bookmarks=0,1,48&shareLine=0

summary: SSRF and Token Exfiltration via OIDC Connection String Injection in mongo-rust-driver (https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-rust-driver)
description:
The {{mongo-rust-driver}} implements OIDC authentication for Azure and GCP environments by querying the cloud Instance Metadata Service (IMDS). The driver constructs the IMDS URL by directly interpolating user-supplied values ({{{}TOKEN_RESOURCE{}}} and {{{}username{}}}) into the URL string without URL-encoding. This allows an attacker who controls the connection string (or its properties) to inject arbitrary query parameters or modify the resource being requested. This leads to Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF), allowing an attacker to force the driver to request high-privileged access tokens (e.g., for Azure Resource Manager) and exfiltrate them to a malicious server.

 

Please see SECBUG-618 for reproduction steps and more information.

summary: ClientOptions::selection_criteria isn't consistently used
description: "Option inheritance" is currently implemented by each individual operation needing to specifically check its parent (client/db/collection), either directly or via the {{resolve_options}} macro.  It looks like we've forgotten to do that for various operations (e.g. {{RunCommand}}) and this is going to continue to be a footgun in the future for new operations without changes to how inheritance is implemented.
